name: Leciñena, San Caprasio, y Estación meteorlógica — circular por los Monegros
region_intro: Mountain biking around Leciñena centers on its strategic location bordering the Sierra de Alcubierre, offering a diverse landscape for riders. The region features a network of dirt tracks, ranging from smooth and compact to technical sections, amidst forests and scrubland. This terrain provides varied elevation changes, including ascents to higher parts of the sierra, making it suitable for different skill levels. Riders can expect panoramic views of the surrounding area and distant glimpses of the Pyrenees. Best mountain bike trails around Leciñena The most popular mountain bike trail is Monte Oscuro y San Caprasio — circular por los Montes Pedriguera y la sierra de Alcubierre, a 28.9 miles (46.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 23 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the Montes Pedriguera and the Sierra de Alcubierre. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Leciñena, San Caprasio, y Estación meteorlógica — circular por los Monegros, a difficult 35.0 miles (56.3 km) path. This route offers significant climbs and panoramic views, including parts of the Monegros desert. Local mountain bikers also love the San Caprasio Trail – San Caprasio Viewpoint loop from Perdiguera, a 33.5 miles (53.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain to the San Caprasio Viewpoint, often completed in about 5 hours 11 minutes. Mountain biking around Leciñena is defined by the Sierra de Alcubierre, varied dirt tracks, and historical sites like Civil War defensive positions. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Leciñena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Leciñena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Leciñena?
A: There are over 70 mountain bike trails around Leciñena, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The majority of these, 55 routes, are rated as moderate, with 7 easy and 10 difficult trails also available.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Leciñena?
A: The terrain around Leciñena, particularly within the Sierra de Alcubierre, is diverse. You'll find a network of dirt tracks, ranging from wide, smooth, and compact sections to more technical paths. The landscape features forests, scrubland, and varied elevation changes, including significant ascents to higher parts of the sierra.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Leciñena?
A: Yes, many routes around Leciñena are circular. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983654182/monte-oscuro-y-san-caprasio-circular-por-los-montes-pedriguera-y-la-sierra-de-alcubierre" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Oscuro y San Caprasio — circular por los Montes Pedriguera y la sierra de Alcubierre&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 46.5 km loop. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1147673767/lecinena-san-caprasio-y-estacion-meteorlogica-circular-por-los-monegros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leciñena, San Caprasio, y Estación meteorlógica — circular por los Monegros&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 56.3 km circular path.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural features to look out for on the trails?
A: The trails offer spectacular panoramic views, including the Ésera Valley, surrounding villages, and on clear days, even distant glimpses of the Pyrenees and parts of the Monegros desert. Notable natural features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2963147" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campillo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5753138" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enebro Peak&lt;/a&gt;. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5285101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit of Monte Oscuro&lt;/a&gt; for expansive views.

Q: Are there any historical sites along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the Sierra de Alcubierre region is rich in history. Many routes lead to interesting landmarks such as rural shrines like the Ermita de la Virgen de la Sabina, and preserved remains of Civil War defensive positions, including trenches and lookout posts on hilltops. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17545658" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orwell's Trenches – Posición San Simón loop from Albergue del Santuario de Magallón&lt;/a&gt; is one such route that explores these historical sites.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Leciñena?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations depend on personal preference, the region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for mountain biking, offering milder temperatures. Summer can be hot, especially in the open plains, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable during those months.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Leciñena?
A: The mountain bike trails around Leciñena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 50 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the panoramic views, and the combination of natural beauty with historical intrigue found along the routes.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are 7 easy mountain bike trails around Leciñena. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, the presence of wide, predominantly smooth, and compact dirt tracks, like sections of the 'Leciñena - A123' multi-use trail, suggests suitable options for less experienced riders or families looking for a gentler ride.

Q: Can I find challenging mountain bike routes in Leciñena?
A: Absolutely. Leciñena offers 10 difficult mountain bike trails for experienced riders. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1147673767/lecinena-san-caprasio-y-estacion-meteorlogica-circular-por-los-monegros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leciñena, San Caprasio, y Estación meteorlógica — circular por los Monegros&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6954375" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Caprasio Trail – San Caprasio Viewpoint loop from Perdiguera&lt;/a&gt; feature significant climbs and technical sections, providing a demanding experience.

Q: Are there any caves or unique geological formations to explore?
A: Yes, the area features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5268339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Caprasio Caves&lt;/a&gt;, which can be an interesting point of interest during your mountain biking adventure. Additionally, you might encounter natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2856000" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Sabina de Villamayor&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5624174" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pino Guitón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail in the area?
A: One of the longest and most challenging routes is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1147673767/lecinena-san-caprasio-y-estacion-meteorlogica-circular-por-los-monegros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Leciñena, San Caprasio, y Estación meteorlógica — circular por los Monegros&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 56.3 km and typically takes around 5 hours 24 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any religious or cultural landmarks accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, you can find cultural landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5771132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Magallón&lt;/a&gt;. Routes often pass by or lead to these sites, offering a blend of outdoor activity and cultural exploration.
tips: Legend has it that San Caprasio was a shepherd (probably of goats, given his name) in the Sierra de Guara. One day he decided to become a monk and threw his staff very far, going to fall here, in the Sierra de Alcubierre.

We are at the viewpoint that bears the name of the hermitage of San Caprasio. From here we will have spectacular views of the Ebro valley and other regions that we can distinguish on the information panel.
From the Monte Oscuro weather radar we have magnificent views of the Perdiguera and Farlete mountains. You can also see the beautiful hermitage of San Caprasio carved into the rock.

This small municipality of Leciñena in the Aragonese Monegros has a small population (1150 in 2004) but quite a bit of history. The town played an important role in the reconquest of Zaragoza. Much later, during the Spanish Civil War it was part of the republican resistance until October 12, 1936 when Franco's troops took the town.

The town has a very good hostel where you can enjoy the tranquility. Around Leciñena we can find Sabinas, a tree from the Mesozoic era that, thanks to its properties resistant to extreme temperatures, can coexist with the harsh weather conditions of the area.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of Our Lady of Magallón
